As you depart, consider a refreshing session in the POWER: Gym or join a Pilates class in the PURE: Yoga & Pilates Studio to rejuvenate after your city exploration.

tour_name: Sydney, Uluru & the Great Barrier Reef - Cruise & Land Journey
location: Coffs Harbour, New South Wales, Australia
activity: Coffs Harbour is a laid-back coastal town set between the Great Dividing Range and the Pacific Ocean. Known for its expansive beaches and relaxed atmosphere, it offers a blend of scenic coastline, lush hinterlands, and local character. Explore the surrounding forests, enjoy the serene beaches, or discover the town's playful side at the iconic ‘Big Banana', a landmark that reflects the region's fun-loving spirit. Return on board to unwind with in-suite dining from the 24-hour menu, then bring the day to a close with a nightcap in one of the eight bars or lounges.

tour_name: Sydney, Uluru & the Great Barrier Reef - Cruise & Land Journey
activity: Newcastle offers a compelling blend of coastal charm and cultural character, with sweeping ocean views, heritage architecture, and a growing contemporary arts scene. Scenic Luxury Cruises & Tours was founded in Newcastle, giving the city a special significance for the company. Explore relaxed beachfront promenades, discover vibrant street art and galleries, or enjoy the city's lively café and dining culture, all set against the backdrop of a beautiful natural coastline. This evening, indulge in the Sushi & Saké Bar @ Koko's, or for one last time retreat to the Senses Spa for a deeply restorative experience.

tour_name: Sydney, Uluru & the Great Barrier Reef - Cruise & Land Journey
activity: Your Discovery Voyage concludes this morning in Sydney, Australia's iconic harbour city. Enjoy a final breakfast on board, taking in the views from your suite or private verandah as the skyline comes into view. Disembark and bid farewell to the Scenic Eclipse II crew and fellow guests, taking with you lasting memories of an exceptional voyage along Australia's east coast. #Spa treatments and services at additional cost. Daily activities may be subject to change; please consult your Daily Wonder for updates. +All drinks on board, including those stocked in your mini bar, are included, except for a very small number of rare, fine and vintage wines, Champagnes and spirits. Specialty restaurants require a reservation, enquire on board. ^Experiences on board our two helicopters and submersible are at additional cost, subject to regulatory approval, availability, weight restrictions, medical approval and weather conditions. ~Kayak and stand-up paddleboard not available in all locations. The itinerary is a guide only and may be amended for operational reasons. As such Scenic cannot guarantee the voyage will operate unaltered from the itinerary stated above. Please refer to our terms and conditions for further information.

When you arrive at Ayers Rock Airport, make your way to the front of the airport terminal for your complimentary transfer to your hotel. Late afternoon you'll be driven to the Uluru Sunset Viewing Area*. Stroll around the viewing area to discover your own spot to witness this magical event. Enjoy sparkling wine as the sun sets over the western horizon and the evening sky brings out the many shades of colour for which Uluru is renowned. As the sky transitions to twilight and you return to the resort, reflect on the experience of the desert and the grandeur of Uluru. *If you wish to take home Aboriginal artwork, please ensure you have cash for your purchases. Please note the purchase of artwork is not always possible as the Anangu may be absent for cultural reasons.

This morning rise early to travel to the base of Uluru accompanied by your driver/guide, commence the Uluru Base walk at Sunrise. Beginning at Kuniya Piti, a sacred site, hear the Tjukurpa (Anangu – the local Aboriginal people - beliefs and law) story of the Mala Men. Continue anti-clockwise around Uluru to the Mala Walk, one of the most important habitation areas for the local Aboriginal people over thousands of years. The Mala walk includes a visit into Kantju Gorge, where after rainfall, water can cascade over the side of Uluru and plunge some 90 metres. After a short rest enjoy a light breakfast before continuing to the Lungkata Walk where your guide will tell you the Tjukurpa story of the cheeky Blue Tongue Lizard! Next stop is the Mutitjulu Waterhole, once an important water source for the Anangu people. Your guide will indicate the natural features of the landscape, Aboriginal rock paintings and relay more traditional stories relating to this special place. Finally walk back to Kuniya Piti where your walk began and board your air-conditioned coach back for the short trip back to Ayers Rock Resort.

Travel from Ayers Rock Resort to Kings Canyon, stopping for breakfast (included) along the way. Make the rocky climb to the rim of the canyon for marvellous views of Watarrka National Park. Follow the rim of the canyon stopping to look down into the canyon gorge at look-out points. Time permitting, you may choose to descend into the green oasis of the 'Garden of Eden'. The circuit walk takes approximately 3 hours to complete and is rocky and steep in parts. It is recommended for those with a good level of fitness. Alternatively, you may wish to take an easier, shorter walk to explore the boulder strewn canyon floor. After lunch (own expense), return to Ayers Rock Resort.

location: Uluru Sunrise & Kata Tjuta - Depart Uluru
activity: Rise early this morning to travel to Talinguru Nyakunytjaku, the Uluru Sunrise Viewing Area. Watch the first rays of sun light up the desert while enjoying a warming cup of tea or coffee. After sunrise travel to the mystical 36 domes of Kata Tjuta. On the way, enjoy panoramic views of the southern side of Kata Tjuta at a dune viewing platform before arriving at Walpa Gorge. Spend some time exploring the gorge and the unusual conglomerate rock formations. The 1.3km walking trail through Walpa Gorge follows the natural creek between two of the tallest domes of Kata Tjuta.

tour_name: Walking Discoveries of Lord Howe Island’s Flora, Seabirds & Marine Life
location: Sydney to Lord Howe Island, Curators Museum Tour and Pre-Dinner Lecture on Geology, Flora & Fauna
activity: Our flight departs from Sydney early this morning. Arrive on Lord Howe Island, where you will be transferred to your accommodation before taking a curator's tour of the local museum. This evening's pre-dinner lecture will feature an overview of the region's geology, flora, fauna and conservation.

tour_name: Walking Discoveries of Lord Howe Island’s Flora, Seabirds & Marine Life
location: Lord Howe History and Sights, Stevens Reserve and Kentia Palms Walk
activity: Today's island exploration will include a bus tour to see Lord Howe Island's many landmarks and to learn about its history. You'll also immerse yourself in nature during a walk through Stevens Reserve, while also learning about the Kentia palm industry. This evening enjoy a lecture on teh Plants of Lord Howe Island. Walk: 2.5 Kilometres, approximately 2 Hours

tour_name: Walking Discoveries of Lord Howe Island’s Flora, Seabirds & Marine Life
location: Clear Place Walk to see Ball's Pyramid and Ned’s Coral Beach Reef Walk
activity: This morning, Ian will guide you to Clear Place, where you'll get to witness the tall palm forest, massive banyan trees and array of seabirds that are found here. After lunch, at low tide, set off on a reef walk at Ned's Beach rock platform to discover the surrounding coral and rockpools. As some of the world's most southerly, the coral found here is rich and diverse, and among the last to be impacted by global environmental effects such as coral bleaching. You'll also learn about the wealth of marine creatures that inhabit the island's intertidal areas. You then have free time for swimming or to explore the local shops. Walk: 5 Kilometres, approximately 3 Hours

tour_name: Walking Discoveries of Lord Howe Island’s Flora, Seabirds & Marine Life
location: Transit Hill and Glass Bottom Boat Cruise and Snorkelling
activity: After breakfast you will set off on a walk to Transit Hill where there is a viewing platform that offers a 360-degree view of Lord Howe Island. Look out for the island's signature plants, the Kentia and curly palms that dominate the landscape in many places. Later in the day, take a glass-bottom boat cruise, discovering the marine life supported by the world's most southerly coral reef. You'll also have the option to snorkel and see these marine wonders up close. Walk: 3 Kilometres, approximately 3 Hours

tour_name: Walking Discoveries of Lord Howe Island’s Flora, Seabirds & Marine Life
location: Little Island to the Base of Mount Gower and Historical Movie
activity: This morning, take a walk along the south-western shoreline to Little Island, located at the base of Lord Howe's mountains. Here, you'll learn about the volcanic origins of the island while soaking up the spectacular scenery of this World Heritage-listed site. Enjoy an afternoon at leisure to relax or explore before a showing of historical movies at the public hall. Walk: 3 Kilometres, approximately 2 Hours

tour_name: Walking Discoveries of Lord Howe Island’s Flora, Seabirds & Marine Life
location: North Bay – Old Gulch and Mt Eliza Walks
activity: Today, set off on a day trip to North Bay. Travelling by boat, you'll arrive at a pristine beach on the northern tip of the island, inaccessible by car. The waters here are a snorkeller's paradise; rich in colourful corals, turtles, stingrays and an estimated 500 species of fish. The birdlife is also spectacular. Look out for colonies of sooty terns, brown noddies and black noddies during a short nature walk. You may also spot red-tailed tropicbirds and waders during low tide. Enjoy a picnic lunch before returning to your accommodation later this afternoon. Walk: 5 Kilometres, approximately 3 Hours

tour_name: Walking Discoveries of Lord Howe Island’s Flora, Seabirds & Marine Life
activity: After breakfast, you'll be transferred to the airport for your return flight to Sydney.

location: Board The Indian Pacific In Perth
activity: Your transcontinental adventure begins in Perth, where you'll gather with fellow travellers for a warm welcome and a celebratory start to your journey. As the Indian Pacific readies to depart, anticipation builds for the days ahead – crossing the country from ocean to ocean, along the world's longest stretch of straight railway track. With the sun dipping low over the west, settle into your cabin, meet new friends in the lounge, and savour your first dinner on board.

Awake to see the sunrise across the vast Nullarbor before arriving in Kalgoorlie, Australia's largest outback town. This morning, delve into the region's fascinating past and present with a visit to its gold-rush heritage and the modern-day Super Pit mine. Choose your Off Train Experience to explore Kalgoorlie in your own way, with morning refreshments included. Reboarding mid-morning for brunch, spend the afternoon at leisure on board. Relax with fine hospitality as the endless sweep of the Nullarbor passes by. Crossing borders and time zones, you'll arrive late evening in the near-ghost town of Cook, South Australia. Once bustling, today only a handful of residents remain, yet its stories endure. Step into this outback outpost to explore the ghost-like town, and as evening falls look skyward – here, under a canopy of stars unspoiled by light, you'll join a guided stargazing experience to spot the Southern Cross and other constellations that shine bright with dazzling clarity. Later, return to the train for a regionally inspired dinner and the gentle rhythm of the rails as you continue eastward.

location: Indian Pacific, Adelaide And Monarto Safari Lodge
activity: Spend the morning travelling through the pastoral heart of South Australia, with sweeping views of the Flinders Ranges, Spencer Gulf, and the Adelaide Plains unfolding beyond your window. A leisurely breakfast and lunch are served on board, inviting you to savour the relaxed rhythm of train travel. Mid-afternoon, the train arrives in Adelaide where you will be transferred by private vehicle to Monarto Safari Lodge, approximately 1 hour from the city centre and nestled in South Australia's expansive Murraylands. Monarto Safari Lodge offers an extraordinary escape into nature's embrace, combining luxurious Safari Tents with authentic experiences, during your stay you'll be surrounded by Africa's wildest animals. With some free time before checking in, you may choose to laze by the Lodge plunge pools, enjoy a game of pickle ball or indulge in the luxurious day spa at Monarto Safari Resort. Settle into your luxurious Safari Tent and enjoy the views as far as the eye can see. This evening dine at the Lodge delighting in a three course menu carefully curated by the onsite chef.

This morning breakfast is served in the Lodge before heading out to explore Monarto Safari Park via the guided Zu-Loop bus experience, or a combination of the Zu-Loop bus and walking. Monarto is the largest open-range safari experience in the world outside of Africa and spans more than 1,500 hectares and is home to more than 50 species of exotic and native mammals, birds and reptiles. You can hop on and off the bus at the wildlife viewing platforms and spend time observing your favourite animal species. Additionally, you can enhance your visit by attending Daily Keeper Presentations and booking a range of intimate behind-the-scenes animal experiences. Early evening, join the Sunset Wildlife Safari, soaking in the last rays of sun on your small group guided tour. Enjoy exclusive access to Monarto Safari Park's Wild Africa area after hours with ample opportunity to immerse yourself in the vast parklands and watch the free-roaming herds of African animals across 550 hectares. As you explore the lands from the comfort of a custom built Safari Landcruiser, you'll have the opportunity to observe and photograph animals including Southern White Rhinos, Giraffes, Cheetahs, Eland, Ostriches, and Zebras. Light refreshments will be served during the tour, capping off the unforgettable experience. Tonight you may choose to dine at the Lodge or in the Resort restaurant, Kutjera.
Today, your wild Monarto Luxury Stay comes to an end. After breakfast, check out of your accommodation and transfer by private vehicle to your choice of the Adelaide Airport or Adelaide CBD Hotel.

tour_name: Great Barrier Reef Discovery with Tropical Queensland Highlights
activity: Arrive in the sun-drenched haven of Cairns, where your journey to paradise begins. A scenic transfer will take you to the luxurious Sheraton Grand in Port Douglas. Find paradise as you are welcomed by the calming sounds of rolling waves blowing a gentle breeze through the tropical palms and vibrant gardens of the resort. This evening, savour a flavourful degustation dinner at Harrisons.

tour_name: Great Barrier Reef Discovery with Tropical Queensland Highlights
activity: Explore Mossman Gorge in the Daintree Rainforest and set off on the Ngadiku Dreamtime Gorge Walk with knowledgeable Indigenous guides. After crossing the Daintree River, immerse yourself in the rainforest's magic, wandering along enchanting boardwalks and trails through its otherworldly landscape. Experience a traditional smoking ceremony, gain exclusive access to Kuku Yalanji land, and discover the rich stories and traditions of Kuku Yalanji culture. Enjoy a soap leaf and ochre demonstration, savour bush tea, and taste freshly baked damper. Your visit also includes a Day Pass Shuttle Bus transfer from Mossman Gorge Centre to Mossman Gorge National Park.

tour_name: Great Barrier Reef Discovery with Tropical Queensland Highlights
activity: Today, embark on a Daintree River cruise to spot incredible wildlife, including formidable saltwater crocodiles, birdlife and other rainforest inhabitants. Later, journey to the Atherton Tablelands, a stunning region where lush rainforests, serene wetlands and expansive savanna landscapes converge. Here, you'll have the opportunity to view the impressive Curtain Fig Tree, whose extensive aerial roots cascade down some 15 metres to the forest floor, creating a striking ‘curtain' effect. This evening, enjoy a relaxed dinner at “The Big Pub” in Malanda, a local favourite known for its hearty meals and welcoming country charm.

tour_name: Great Barrier Reef Discovery with Tropical Queensland Highlights
activity: Today, step into the enchanting world of Paronella Park, a magical place renowned for its captivating ruins, lush gardens, and rich history. As you stroll through the park at your own pace, uncover the fascinating story behind its creation by Spanish visionary, José Paronella, who dreamt of building a castle in the heart of the rainforest. Explore the romantic ruins, meander through charming gardens, and marvel at the park's hidden treasures, from the grand staircase to the awe-inspiring waterfall that adds to its mystical allure. Indulge in a delightful lunch surrounded by the park's natural beauty, where the sound of the cascading waterfall and the rustling of the leaves create a serene atmosphere. In the evening, return to Cairns and enjoy a dinner at Dundee's on the Waterfront.

tour_name: Great Barrier Reef Discovery with Tropical Queensland Highlights
activity: Today, immerse yourself in the beauty of the Wet Tropics, a World Heritage-listed region of ancient rainforest, mountain ranges and cascading waterfalls. Indulge in a Signature Experience. Then, return to Cairns via the Kuranda Scenic Railway in your Gold Class cabin. Enjoy a local produce cheese platter served with a tropical welcome drink. Enjoy a sumptuous dinner at the hotel, allowing you to unwind and savour the exquisite flavours of this coastal paradise. Signature Experience - Skyrail Rainforest Cableway & Kuranda Scenic Railway Embark on a breathtaking journey aboard the Skyrail Rainforest Cableway, where you'll glide over the ancient rainforest with unparalleled views. Arrive in the charming Kuranda Village, known for its vibrant markets, art and crafts. Nestled in the heart of Kuranda, just a short stroll from the Skyrail and Kuranda Scenic Railway stations, is a haven for over 1,200 butterflies representing diverse species at the Australian Butterfly Sanctuary. As you meander through the aviary's picturesque boardwalks and lush gardens, you'll be captivated by these delicate marvels of nature.

tour_name: Great Barrier Reef Discovery with Tropical Queensland Highlights
activity: Enjoy a relaxed breakfast and free time today before embarking your cruise in the afternoon. Step aboard the Coral Discoverer for your 7-night journey to the remote and untouched Northern reefs. Settle in on board as the ship anchors off Double Island. Explore the ship's facilities before joining the captain for welcome drinks on the Sun Deck.

tour_name: Great Barrier Reef Discovery with Tropical Queensland Highlights
activity: Step ashore in historic Cooktown and delve into the rich heritage of the Guugu Yimithirr, the traditional custodians of the land. Explore a chapter of European history at the site where lieutenant James Cook grounded the HMB Endeavour for repairs in 1770. Stroll through the beautiful flora and the Botanic Gardens, and visit the Cooktown Museum, where you'll learn about Cook's landing from an Aboriginal viewpoint, complemented by fascinating artefacts. Later, relax on board or enjoy the ship's amenities as we cruise toward Lizard Island.

tour_name: Great Barrier Reef Discovery with Tropical Queensland Highlights
activity: Immerse yourself in the beauty of Lizard Island with a day of exploration. Delight at the colourful marine life as you snorkel among vibrant reefs, marvelling at the giant clam gardens at Watson's Bay, or simply unwind on the soft white sands beneath the shade of casuarina trees, enjoying the fresh sea breeze and soaking up the island's tranquil charm.

tour_name: Great Barrier Reef Discovery with Tropical Queensland Highlights
activity: Today, seize the rare opportunity to snorkel the stunning Ribbon Reefs. Stretching 50 to 100 km from the mainland and spanning 200 km from Cooktown to Lizard Island, this chain of narrow reefs - numbered from #1 to #10 - marks the dramatic meeting point between the expansive coral ribbons and the deep, mysterious waters of the open ocean as the continental shelf falls away. Join a guided snorkel tour with our expert Expedition Team, and immerse yourself in the vibrant underwater world teeming with marine life.

tour_name: Great Barrier Reef Discovery with Tropical Queensland Highlights
activity: Today, we continue our in-depth exploration of the Ribbon Reefs, delving further into this extraordinary underwater paradise. Each site offers its own unique features, from intricate coral gardens and impressive drop-offs to bustling schools of fish and the chance to spot larger marine creatures like manta rays and reef sharks.

tour_name: Great Barrier Reef Discovery with Tropical Queensland Highlights
location: Escape Reef, Mackay Reef and Cay
activity: Visit Escape Reef for an unforgettable snorkelling experience where you'll find an underwater world that boasts an array of flourishing coral and wildlife. Then, it's on to Mackay Reef, where its northern side benefits from extra protection provided by the nearby cay, creating a haven for turtles. This area is also home to giant sea clams, some growing as large as 1.2 metres, each showcasing their own unique colours and patterns.

tour_name: Great Barrier Reef Discovery with Tropical Queensland Highlights
activity: Today, enjoy the serenity of Fitzroy Island. Wander through the enchanting Secret Garden rainforest, or for something more adventurous, you might choose to head to the de-commissioned lighthouse where you'll find unparalleled views of Coral Sea, islands and the mainland mountain ranges. Snorkel the fringing reef, bathe in the sun on the beach, and experience the unique opportunity to assist the volunteers at the Cairns Turtle Rehabilitation Centre, where they care for sick and/or injured turtles for releasing them back into their natural habitat. In the afternoon, arrive at Sudbury Cay, a secluded and tiny sand cay slightly emerged from the Coral Sea. Take off your shoes and feel the sand granules under your feet while gentle tropical waves caress the shore. Relax with sundowner drinks with your fellow adventurers as you watch the glorious sunset.

tour_name: Great Barrier Reef Discovery with Tropical Queensland Highlights
activity: Your journey concludes upon arrival in Cairns. After enjoying a final breakfast on board, you'll disembark and bid your fellow travellers farewell.

This evening, get to know your fellow travellers, Driver-Guide and Tour Director during a special Signature Experience. Signature Experience - Darwin Sunset Dinner Cruise See Darwin in a whole new light when you embark on a dinner cruise at sunset, setting off along the harbour for an evening of panoramic views and a delicious buffet feast made with locally sourced produce.

location: Darwin, Litchfield National Park, Adelaide River
activity: Discover Litchfield National Park and visit both Wangi Falls and Florence Falls, set among a monsoon forest. After lunch, continue to Katherine. Tonight enjoy dinner at your hotel.

On the way to Lorella Springs, visit the idyllic Mataranka Thermal Pool, a natural oasis where the warm, crystal-clear waters are rumoured to possess healing properties for aches and pains. View the springs and and enjoy the serene beauty of the Australian outback. Arrive at Lorella Springs Wilderness Park, a sprawling one-million-acre working cattle station, and enjoy dinner at the property.

Explore the natural surrounds of Lorella Springs Wilderness Park today. Join your guide for a scenic walk through several of the park's trails and perhaps go for a dip in one of the waterholes or thermal springs. In the afternoon, embark on a scenic helicopter flight over the Lost City. This evening, watch the sunset over the property before enjoying dinner. Signature Experience - Lost City Scenic Flight Board a scenic flight over the Lost City, a series of large sandstone outcrops that evoke the remains of an ancient civilisation.
Today, spend a full day exploring the pristine wilderness. Your guide will lead you along bush tracks, as well as swim in the waterholes and relax in the thermal springs.

location: Lorella Wilderness Park, Borroloola, Hell's Gate
activity: After your morning in Lorella Springs, travel to Borroloola, where you will board your scenic flight to Hell's Gate.

Arrive at Burketown Thermal Springs, hot springs that have been created from a bore drilled into the ground in 1897. Continue on to Burke and Wills Camp 119, which was the most northerly campsite during the Burke and Wills expedition in 1861. Afterwards, you will visit Leichhardt Falls, a plunging waterfall on the Leichhardt River. Take in the natural landscape, and you may even spot a saltwater crocodile in the nearby river.

Journey to the historic goldrush town of Croydon, the heritage capital of the Gulf and visit the True Blue Interactive Information Centre. Take yourself on a self-guided walking tour of the Croydon town. Enjoy dinner at your hotel tonight.

Discover Cobbold Gorge, hidden away within a spectacularly rugged sandstone formation. This morning, enjoy a boat cruise and tour through these narrow waterways as a guide points out the geological features of this incredible natural wonder. Remember to keep watch for the freshwater crocodiles.

After breakfast this morning, continue to Undara Volcanic National Park and set out on a guided discovery of the intriguing lava tubes. Signature Experience - Undara Lava Tubes Embark on the Archway Explorer tour through the mysterious Undara Lava Tubes. Be guided through the mesmerising lava tubes, the result of a volcanic eruption that spewed molten lava down a dry riverbed. The outer layer formed a crust, and when the lava below drained away, a series of long, hollow tubes remained.

location: Undara, Millstream Falls, Herberton Historic Town, Cairns
activity: Today, travel from Ravenshoe to Herberton and onwards to Cairns. Enjoy a visit and talk at Woodleigh Station, which is located within the surrounds of the quintessential Australian bush. Journey to the beautiful tiered waterfall, Millstream Falls. Later visit Herberton Historic Village before finishing the day in Cairns with a dinner at your hotel.
Your adventure draws to a close after breakfast this morning. Be transferred to the airport for your onward flight.

location: Depart Sydney For Canberra, Australia's Capital
activity: Dive into five days of history, food, and sightseeing on your Australia tour package in quaint Berrima before arriving in Gold Creek Station. Experience a true taste of Aussie hospitality from the Starr Family who'll offer insights into farm life on their 400-hectare station. Watch Craig give a sheep shearing demonstration before they get to cooking and fire up the BBQ for a Be My Guest BBQ lunch and tales about life on the land. This is your entrée to the country's cool little capital of Canberra, home to Parliament House where you can explore the grounds or take a self-guided tour. After a full first day, get to know your group over a drink at your hotel.

Venture out of the city and see it from above ascending the summit of towering Mt. Ainsley. The views are your perfect farewell to Canberra before crossing the mighty Murray River into the state of Victoria to take a step back in time and Dive Into Culture in one of Australia's best-preserved gold rush towns, Beechworth. Wherever you explore in the Beechworth Historic Precinct, there's plenty to see in the many historic buildings preserved from the gold rush days as well as in the honey and gold shops, galleries, and museums. Get the lowdown on the town over a chat with the publican of the historic local pub and enjoy afternoon tea in the oldest hotel in town. After a taste of the local fare, you'll travel to the nearby city of Wangaratta.

It's a complete change of scene as we journey to cosmopolitan Melbourne where you'll get your bearings on a city sightseeing tour. Discover why Melbourne has justifiably earned its reputation as one of the world's most livable cities and delve into its Victorian history in the CBD, Southbank's sprawling riverfront, arts district, and natural beauty at the Botanic Gardens. Immerse yourself in a MAKE TRAVEL MATTER® Experience on a guided galleries tour of our First Peoples arts and cultural centre, the Koorie Heritage Trust Gallery. Cruise by the Crown complex and consider coming back this evening for one of its super-chef's restaurants, designer boutiques, or indoor amusement park. After seeing so much of what awaits, you'll no doubt be eager to explore more of this stylish city. Fortunately, you're staying in a centrally located area, opposite - a fun area that comes alive at night. Pop back to an area you'd like to get to know better or tuck into a delicious seafood dinner and watch the sunset on the banks of the Yarra.

It's your last full day in Melbourne and you'll want to make the most of it exploring on your own or joining an Optional Experience in or outside of the city. If you're looking for a view, Melbourne Skydeck at the Eureka Tower, is the place for panoramic, floor-to-ceiling vistas of the city. Or perhaps exploring the city on foot with a History and Donuts Walking Tour is more your thing. Another option is a trip to Phillip Island to see the Little Penguins make their trek from the sea to their sand dune burrow homes - a totally unique wildlife encounter not possible anywhere else in the world. It's hard to believe it's only been four days, and oh what a journey it's been! Back in Melbourne your spoilt for choices to raise a glass and celebrate the things we've tasted, learnt, seen and discovered over the last four days.
All good things must come to an end, as your travels through Southern Australia conclude, say a fond farewell to your fellow travellers before your transfer to Melbourne Airport (flights anytime).

Your East Coast journey begins with a warm welcome at Hanworth House in East Brisbane, where you'll check in gather with fellow travellers for a celebratory start to your summer journey. After setting off from Brisbane on the Great Southern, life on board begins to unfold. Savour lunch as the train winds south, then settle into the rhythm of your cabin and the passing landscapes. In the afternoon, the Great Southern arrives in the coastal town of Coffs Harbour. Here, the evening unfolds in true coastal style. As the sun sinks below the horizon, enjoy drinks and canapés on the sand at Charlesworth Bay before a signature outdoor dinner - weather permitting- showcasing fresh seafood and regional produce paired with local wines. The soundtrack is set by a live band and the gentle rhythm of waves on the shore. After a few unhurried hours, reboard the train and settle in for your first night on board.

location: Hunter Valley, Port Stephens Or Newcastle
activity: Start the day with a hearty breakfast on board before setting off on your choice of Off Train Experiences. Wine lovers can explore the picturesque Hunter Valley, Australia's oldest wine region. Known for Semillon and Shiraz, here you'll spend the day amongst the vines, visiting Tulloch and Brokenwood Wines followed by a platter-style lunch featuring local produce at Twine Restaurant. If you'd prefer to take to the skies, treat yourself to an optional upgrade with a Hunter Valley helicopter flight that soars over the rolling green hills, showcasing the best of the wine region and beyond. Alternatively, discover Port Stephens by land or sea. Venture by 4WD across the vast Stockton Bight Sand Dunes, the largest mobile dunes in the Southern Hemisphere. Stop to admire your surrounds and drive to the top of a dune to take in the sweeping views - you may even choose to sandboard down. On the water tour, be dazzled by turquoise seas as you soak up the coastline on a relaxed boat cruise, with the chance to spot dolphins along the way. Whether by land or sea, after your Port Stephens adventure you'll enjoy a leisurely lunch at Nelson Bay Golf Club before returning to the train in the afternoon. Closer to the city, explore Newcastle on a guided walking tour that reveals its coastal charm, capped with lunch at Scratchleys on the Wharf before returning to the train for a regionally inspired dinner.

Enjoy a leisurely breakfast on board as the Great Southern travels through Victoria's high country before choosing your next Off Train Experience. Victoria is well-known for its excellent food and wine scene, which is on show at Clyde Park in the Moorabool Valley. Clyde Park is a beautiful vineyard, cellar door and bistro set among rolling hills. Here, you'll enjoy a long lunch and sample cool-climate wines while being entertained on the glorious lawns. Alternatively, spend the day in cosmopolitan Melbourne to discover why it's considered the cultural and sporting capital of Australia. Highlights include the Ian Potter Centre at NGV Australia, with its remarkable collection of Australian art, and a behind-the-scenes tour of the iconic Melbourne Cricket Ground, guided by a club member. History buffs may prefer the Old Melbourne Gaol, where stories of the past, including those of Ned Kelly, come to life. To complete your Melbourne experience, take in the sweeping views from Melbourne Skydeck before ascending to Eureka 89 for a curated lunch showcasing seasonal local produce. Afterwards, return to the train to continue your journey south.

Catch the morning light over regional South Australia, a final reminder of the landscapes you've travelled through. Sit back and enjoy a leisurely breakfast on board, savouring the last moments of life on train. Soon, the Great Southern glides into Adelaide, where your journey concludes - leaving you with memories of extraordinary landscapes, shared stories, and experiences that linger long after the train has stopped.

Prepare for a journey of discovery, delving into cultural history and awe-inspiring natural wonders. Arrive in Tasmania's historic capital, Hobart, and be transferred to your hotel. Afterwards, enjoy your selected Freedom of Choice activity. Tonight, delight in a welcome dinner and drink.

location: Russell Falls, Cradle Mountain-Lake St Clair National Park, Strahan
activity: Discover the magic of Russell Falls in Mount Field National Park today, then continue to the World Heritage-listed Cradle Mountain-Lake St Clair National Park, before travelling via the mining town of Queenstown to Strahan. Tonight, savour dinner at View 42° Restaurant & Bar.

From the comfort of the Captain's Premier Upper Deck, glide across Macquarie Harbour and enter the Franklin-Gordon Wild Rivers National Park. Stop at Heritage Landing, for a guided rainforest walk. Back on board, you'll be treated to a delicious lunch of fresh Tasmanian produce before arriving at Tasmania's World Heritage-listed Sarah Island. Here you'll get a chilling insight into the cruelties of convict life as you explore the ruins of what was once a convict settlement. Cruise past Hells Gates then return to Strahan. Signature Experience - Spirit of the Wild Cruise Board the luxurious Spirit of the Wild for a cruise along the secluded wilderness highway, the Gordon River. From the comfort of the Captain's Premier Upper Deck, glide across Macquarie Harbour and enter the Franklin-Gordon Wild Rivers National Park.
Travel from Strahan to the incredible Cradle Mountain. Then, enjoy your exciting Freedom of Choice experience.

This morning, delight in your Signature Experience with a guided tour of Cradle Mountain - Lake St Clair National Park. Later, enjoy a visit to the unique Tasmanian conservation sanctuary, Devils @ Cradle, home to Tasmanian devils, and spotted-tail and eastern quolls. While here, a keeper will walk you through the sanctuary, providing an insight into the importance of the sanctuary's conservation programs. Signature Experience - Guided Dove Lake Tour Explore beautiful Dove Lake and its surrounds with a friendly local guide, admiring the unique wilderness of Cradle Mountain-Lake St Clair National Park.

Today, travel from Cradle Mountain and stop in Sheffield. Then, relish a winter truffle Signature Experience at a truffle farm. Later, enjoy a historic tour of Franklin House. Built by convicts in 1838, the house was later occupied by one of the colony's leading private schools. Signature Experience - Truffle Farm Visit Visit Australia's first black truffle farm for a group demonstration of dogs actively truffle hunting in the plantation. Afterwards, enjoy some truffle pizza or a truffle toastie in a charming rustic shed. You may like to sample some truffle ice cream for dessert.

This morning embark on a guided tour of Beaconsfield Mine, before indulging in a delightful Signature Experience in a beautiful setting at Josef Chromy Wines. The remainder of the afternoon will be enjoyed at your leisure. Signature Experience - Josef Chromy Wines Visit Josef Chromy Wines, a winery that prides itself on celebrating Tasmanian grapes. Here you'll be treated to a specially prepared lunch and have the chance to sample some of the winery's finest drops.

location: Launceston, Bridestowe Lavender Estate, Coles Bay
activity: After visiting Bridestowe Lavender Estate, traverse Weldborough Pass and admire the views of forested hills and farmland en route to Freycinet National Park on Tasmania's stunning east coast. This evening is yours to enjoy at leisure. Dinner will be served at your lodge. Signature Experience - Bridestowe Lavender Estate Discover the largest lavender farm in the Southern Hemisphere, renowned for its stunning lavender fields. Enjoy a tour of the distillery and savour distinctive lavender-infused delicacies.

After breakfast, visit Freycinet National Park Visitor Centre. Then soak in more stunning coastal scenery during a guided tour along Cape Tourville before returning to Bicheno for your Signature Experience. Signature Experience - Waubs Harbour Distillery Tour and Whisky Tasting Experience a full distillery tour and whisky tasting at Waubs Harbour. Located on the rocky shores of Bicheno, on Tasmania's east coast, the distillery was formerly an oyster hatchery. With five salt-soaked bond rooms and a cooling system powered by the ocean, the facility is a blend of tradition and innovation.

Visit the historic towns of Ross and Richmond on your way to Hobart. After arriving in the Tasmanian capital, embark on a guided discovery of the city, which takes you through the Royal Tasmanian Botanical Gardens and the heritage district of Battery Point.

Today, explore the Port Arthur Historic Site, Australia's most intact, evocative and notorious convict prison, on a guided tour. See the famous sandstone Penitentiary, then visit the Convict Church and the Separate Prison. You'll then embark on a cruise around the Isle of the Dead and hear haunting stories about the convicts who were transported here from across the world and eventually buried at Port Arthur. This evening, enjoy a farewell dinner at Mures Upper Deck.

This morning, stroll through the Salamanca Market and peruse the treasures of Tasmania at more than 300 stalls. Wander through the array of goods from hand-crafted jewellery and carved wooden trinkets to tantalising Tasmanian gin. Afterwards, bid a fond farewell to the Apple Isle. We say goodbye before we transfer you to the airport for your flight. Enrich Your Experience - Bruny Island Cruise As an alternative to today's touring, you might like to take an award-winning eco-cruise along the rugged Bruny Island coastline.

Upon arrival in Perth at your CBD hotel, you will be met by your Tour Director. This afternoon, enjoy an introduction to the city of Perth as we take you on an informative tour of the city, including Kings Park, with the opportunity to take a walk through the Botanical Gardens. In the evening, share a special welcome dinner at Reel Restaurant overlooking the Swan River.

Spend the morning exploring Margaret River township before visiting Gabriel Chocolate, Western Australia's first bean-to-bar maker, to sample their creations and learn about the process. Next, take a self-guided tour of Mammoth Cave to see ancient fossils and a 50,000-year-old “zygomaturus” jawbone. Later, visit Yallingup to enjoy stunning coastal views and a stroll along the beach. Dinner tonight is at a local restaurant.

location: Margaret River - Busselton - Fremantle - Rottnest Island
activity: We head to Busselton to walk its famous 1.7-kilometre jetty. You'll then travel north through Bunbury and Mandurah, stopping for a fish and chip lunch on the beach. Continuing to Fremantle, board the Rottnest Express ferry to Wadjemup, Rottnest Island. Explore its history, wildlife, and scenic beauty, including 10,000 quokkas, during your 2-night stay.

Today, explore Rottnest Island, known for its 63 beaches and friendly quokkas. After a relaxed morning, take a guided bus tour to the island's highlights, with the chance to spot a quokka or two. After a delicious picnic lunch, spend the afternoon at your leisure. Dinner tonight is at the hotel with fellow travellers, reflecting on your adventure with Outback Spirit.

After breakfast at the hotel, board the Rottnest Express ferry back to Fremantle. When you arrive, you'll rejoin the Outback Spirit coach for a transfer to the Perth Airport where your exploration of Perth, Margaret River and Rottnest Island comes to an end.

Victoria is well-known for its excellent food and wine scene, which is on show at Clyde Park in the Moorabool Valley. Clyde Park is a beautiful vineyard, cellar door and bistro set among rolling hills. Here, you'll enjoy a long lunch and sample cool-climate wines while being entertained on the glorious lawns. location: Great Southern, Adelaide And Monarto Safari Lodge
activity: Catch the morning light over regional South Australia, a final reminder of the landscapes you've travelled through. Sit back and enjoy a leisurely breakfast on board, savouring the last moments of life on train. Soon, the Great Southern glides into Adelaide, where you will be transferred by private vehicle to Monarto Safari Lodge, approximately 1 hour from the city centre and nestled in South Australia's expansive Murraylands. Monarto Safari Lodge offers an extraordinary escape into nature's embrace, combining luxurious Safari Tents with authentic experiences, during your stay you'll be surrounded by Africa's wildest animals. With some free time before checking in, you may choose to laze by the Lodge plunge pools, enjoy a game of pickle ball or indulge in the luxurious day spa at Monarto Safari Resort. Settle into your luxurious Safari Tent and enjoy the views as far as the eye can see. This evening dine at the Lodge delighting in a three course menu carefully curated by the onsite chef.

Monarto is the largest open-range safari experience in the world outside of Africa and spans more than 1,500 hectares and is home to more than 50 species of exotic and native mammals, birds and reptiles. You can hop on and off the bus at the wildlife viewing platforms and spend time observing your favourite animal species. Additionally, you can enhance your visit by attending Daily Keeper Presentations and booking a range of intimate behind-the-scenes animal experiences. Early evening, join the Sunset Wildlife Safari, soaking in the last rays of sun on your small group guided tour. Enjoy exclusive access to Monarto Safari Park's Wild Africa area after hours with ample opportunity to immerse yourself in the vast parklands and watch the free-roaming herds of African animals across 550 hectares. As you explore the lands from the comfort of a custom built Safari Landcruiser, you'll have the opportunity to observe and photograph animals including Southern White Rhinos, Giraffes, Cheetahs, Eland, Ostriches, and Zebras. Light refreshments will be served during the tour, capping off the unforgettable experience. Tonight you may choose to dine at the Lodge or in the Resort restaurant, Kutjera.
Today, your wild Monarto Luxury Stay comes to an end. After breakfast, check out of your accommodation and transfer by private vehicle to your choice of the Adelaide Airport or Adelaide CBD Hotel.
